uk: Всіх святих українського народу. Глас 1. Єв. 2.
saints.en: 1st Sunday after Pentecost. Sunday of All Saints. Hieromartyr Theodotus (Bohdan), Bishop of Ancyra

rubrics:
Вечірня: „Блажен муж”. На “Господи взиваю” – 10 стихир: 4 воскресних і 6 Святих; Слава: Святих, І нині: богородичний-догмат 3-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня і 3 читання Святим: Сир. 34:13-17. Сир. 1:11-21. Муд. 3:1-9. Стихири на литії – Святим. Стиховня воскресна, Слава: Святим, І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом Сл. Наблагословенні хлібів – тропар „Богородице Діво” (2) і Святим (1).
Утреня: по „Бог Господь”: тропар воскресний (2), Слава: Святим, І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом Сл. Катизми чергові і сідальний недільний, полієлей, величання, „Ангельський собор”. Степенна 1-го гласу. Прокімен воскресний. Єв. – Мр. 70 зач.; 16, 1-8. Псалом 50. Канон воскресний, хрестовоскресний, Богородиці і Святих. Катавасія: «Відкрию уста мої». На 9-ій – „Чеснішу”; по 9-ій – „Свят Господь Бог наш” (3) і світильний воскресний, Слава: Святих, І нині: їхній богород. Хвалитних стихир – 8: 5 воскресних і 3 Святих з двома їхніми приспівами; Слава: євангельська стихира, І нині: „Преблагословенна”. Після великого славослов'я – тропар «Сьогодні спасіння світу». Відпуст великий з помин. недільним і Святих.
Часи: тропар воскресний, Слава: Святих, кондак – тільки Святих.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний і Святих; Слава, і нині: кондак Святих. Прокімен недільний гласу і Святих; алилуя Святих, апостол-євангелія воскресний і Святих. Ряд.: Ап. – Рим. 81 зач.; 2, 10-16. Єв. – Мт. 9 зач.; 4, 18-23. Літ.: Свята: Ап. – Рим. 99 зач.; 8, 28-39. Єв. – Мт. 10 зач.; 5, 1-16. Причасний „Хваліте Господа” і „Радуйтеся праведні”.

troparion_en.text: Adorned in the blood of Your Martyrs throughout all the world,  / as if clothed in purple and linen, /  through them Your Church cries out to You, O Christ God:  / “Bestow Your bounties upon Your people,  / grant peace to Your habitation, and great mercy to our souls.”

kontakion_en.text:
(Original Melody)
As the first fruits of nature offered to the Planter of all creation, O Lord,  / the inhabited earth brings the God-bearing Martyrs. /  By their supplications, and the intercession of the Theotokos, /  preserve Your Church in profound peace, O greatly Merciful One.

troparion.text: Adorned in the blood of Your Martyrs throughout all the world,  / as if clothed in purple and linen, /  through them Your Church cries out to You, O Christ God:  / “Bestow Your bounties upon Your people,  / grant peace to Your habitation, and great mercy to our souls.”

kontakion.text:
(Original Melody)
As the first fruits of nature offered to the Planter of all creation, O Lord,  / the inhabited earth brings the God-bearing Martyrs. /  By their supplications, and the intercession of the Theotokos, /  preserve Your Church in profound peace, O greatly Merciful One.

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God “The Unbreakable Wall”

troparion.text: Let us, sinful and humbled, now run earnestly to the Theotokos, /  and let us fall down in repentance, crying from the depths of our soul: /  "Help us, O Sovereign Lady, and have compassion on us. /  Make haste, for we perish from the multitude of our sins. /  Do not turn your servants empty away, for we have you as our only hope.

kontakion.text:
We have no other help, we have no other hope, but you, O Sovereign Lady.  /  Help us, for in you
do we hope,  /  and of you do we boast, for we are your servants.  /  Let us not be put to shame.

oca_saints.name: "Seven Arrows" Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Soften our evil hearts, O Theotokos, and repel the attacks of those who hate us,  /  and loosen all the rigidity of our soul.  /  For beholding your holy image,  /  we are filled with compunction by your compassion and loving kindness toward us,  /  and we kiss your wounds; /   for we are terrified by the darts with which we wound you.  /  O Compassionate Mother,  /  do not let us perish because of our hardness of heart, /   or our neighbor's hardness of heart,  /  for you are truly the Softener of Evil Hearts.

kontakion.text: By your grace, O Sovereign Lady, /   soften the hearts of evildoers,  /  send down benefactors who will preserve them from all evil;  /  with gratitude and zeal, we pray to you before your revered icons.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Theodotus and his companions, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the highest...”)
You struggled well, O Theodotus, / together with your fellow athletes and passion-bearing virgins. / You have received crowns of honor. / Therefore, unceasingly pray to Christ God for us all.

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Marcellinus, Pope of Rome, and those with him

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Sisinius the Deacon of Rome and those with him

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Kyriake, Kaleria, and Mary of Caesarea, in Palestine

troparion.text: As reasonable lambs, you were guided by Christ, the Chief Shepherd, / along the path of martyrdom. / You finished your course and kept the faith; / therefore, honored Kyra, Valerie, and Mary, / with joyful hearts, we magnify Christ today, as we honor your holy memory!

kontakion.text: Passion-bearers Kyra, Valerie and Mary, / you loved the faithful promises of  God; / you clung to the faith of Christ, / looking for eternal life and the blessedness of Paradise! / You endured torture with steadfastness / and bowed your necks beneath the sword. / Therefore, you have been crowned by the hand of the Lord / and glorious is your memory. / Entreat Christ God, the Judge of the contest, / on behalf of those who honor your struggles with faith!

oca_saints.name: Martyr Zēnaίda (Zenais) of Caesarea in Palestine

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Zēnaίda, O Lord, / through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through her intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Zēnaίda;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Daniel, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

(Podoben: “Joseph was amazed..”)
Your lamb Potamiani,  /  O Jesus, cries out in a loud voice:  /  “I love You, my Bridegroom,  / and seeking You, I endure suffering. /  In Your Baptism I was crucified and buried with You, I suffer for Your sake that I might reign with You,  /  and I die for You that I might live with You.  /  Accept me, who with love sacrifice myself for You, as an unblemished offering  / for I have offered myself with love.”  / By her prayers,  O most merciful One, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the things on high..”)
Finding your most revered temple to be a source of healing for our souls, /  we the faithful cry out to you with a loud voice: /  “O most renowned Virgin Martyr Potamiani,  /  entreat Christ God unceasingly on behalf of us all.”
